paper_title: Cretaceous / Paleogene Boundary Analysis by Planktic Foraminiferal Biozonation in the Western Zagros Fold-Thrust Belt (Smaquli valley), Sulaimani Governorate, NE-Iraq

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing samples to identify the K/PG boundary through planktic foraminiferal biozonation.

steps.description: The authors sampled about 230 m of the succession and extended the sampling into part of Maastrichtian and Paleocene, across the Gali section.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from 230 m of geological succession

steps.description: Excavated 152 fresh samples at 1m interval to find the approximate stratigraphic position of the boundary.
After approximate location, 20 additional samples have taken at 10 cm intervals to conduct high-resolution study of the boundary.
steps.searchable_summary: collect 20 additional samples at 10 cm intervals

steps.description: On all samples, the procedure includes picking of the index species and fixing them directionally on slides for identification under stereomicroscopes and electronic scanning.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples by picking index species and fixing on slides

steps.description: The research photographed the described species for further analysis and comparison with previously published photos.
steps.searchable_summary: photograph species for analysis and comparison
